Gray County

Introduction to Gray County

Arrows in Gray County

Arrow #77: Pampa

More Details for Arrow #77

Coordinates: 35.5258411, -100.9777422

Installed: August 7, 2014

Marker Text:

1872-74, Comanche, Kiowa & Cheyenne fought U.S. Army in series of battles on lands in present-day Gray County

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #78: Pampa

More Details for Arrow #78

Coordinates: 35.5348428, -100.9606196

Installed: August 7, 2014

Marker Text:

Comanche, Kiowa & Cheyenne fought Red River War battles near here, seen in White Deer Land Museum exhibit

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #79: McLean

More Details for Arrow #79

Coordinates: 35.2332806, -100.609129

Installed: August 7, 2014

Marker Text:

Comanche & other tribes followed Old Trails Ridge that became mail route, railroad, Route 66 & I-40

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Map of Arrows in Gray County

Explore the map below for arrow locations and the county boundary.

Texas Plains Trail Region Map

Click a county to view arrows

Welcome to the Quanah Parker Trail!

Explore the historical arrow markers commemorating Quanah Parker and the Comanche people across the Texas Plains Trail Region. Use the map on the homepage or navigate via the menu to learn more about this effort.
